sourceMovie.title: The Maze Runner
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Maze Runner (2014) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Wes Ball's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Maze Runner clicked for you, Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Maze Runner?
faq.answer: Try Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials, Insurgent, Maze Runner: The Death Cure—each shares DNA with The Maze Runner while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Maze Runner?
faq.answer: The Maze Runner (2014) is best described as Action, Mystery, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Maze Runner part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Maze Runner Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
